mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic output and higher corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the Spearman correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and equity prices higher over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for about 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: If rank IC of RSAFS monthly change against forward 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, the labor market becomes tight, exerting upward wage pressure and prompting Fed tightening expectations, which depress equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months with UNRATE below 5%, or rank IC for UNRATE changes is positive in that regime over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 2.0% signals the start of monetary tightening, which immediately raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: If average forward 5-day return after FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% is non-negative over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and unemployment is low simultaneously, the economy overheats, forcing the Fed to hike rates rapidly, which sharply depresses stock prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in conditions meeting the trigger, forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative at the 5% level (one-sided t-test) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales outpace industrial production over three months, demand exceeds supply, leading to inventory drawdowns and imported inflation, which dampens corporate profits and equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered months over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (below 2.5%), an increase in rates signals improving economic growth rather than tightening, boosting equities over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical 1992-1994 and 2004-2006 low-rate +xings saw positive stock reactions.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward 20-day returns is negative or zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a recovery regime with unemployment above 5% and still declining, rapidly rising nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic momentum, lifting corporate earnings and stock prices over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: recovery regime (UNRATE > 5% and declining over 3 months)
failure_condition: If in those recovery periods, the rank IC of PAYEMS changes against forward 60-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.
